svg.title: How spatial kernel density estimation creates a continuous intensity surface
svg.desc: A three-step scientific diagram explaining spatial kernel density estimation. The same eighteen observed event locations are shown throughout. First, events are represented as points. Second, every point receives a smooth kernel; three kernels are emphasized for clarity, and bandwidth controls smoothing. Third, the contributions are summed to create an illustrative continuous event-intensity surface. Values have units of events per unit area, not probability or risk. Edge correction and an appropriate exposure denominator may be needed.

text.tspan: Spatial kernel density estimation turns observed point locations into
a smooth surface of estimated event intensity.

Large h produces broader, smoother patterns.
Bandwidth usually affects the result more
